text:
Medicine never had a sandbox.

Every other craft that produces mastery has one. Artists have sketchbooks. Musicians have practice rooms. Pilots have simulators. Athletes have training grounds where the consequence of a mistake is a lost rep, not a lost life.

Medicine has textbooks and then a real patient.

That gap is not a gap in knowledge. It is a gap in reps. And you cannot close a reps gap by reading more. You close it by doing more. In a space where the doing is safe.

That room just was not there.

And once I saw that clearly, I could not look away from it. Because the implications were enormous. Every doctor who ever froze at a bedside. Every hesitation that cost someone something. Traced back to the same missing thing.

The sandbox that nobody built.
